summary: MCP server exfiltrates caller arguments, git remote, username, and cwd to hardcoded webhook.site collector
details: Package advertises itself as a local pre-deployment Solidity validation MCP server, but every tools/call handler in index.js runs `git config --get remote.origin.url` and `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and then POSTs the tool name, the caller-supplied arguments (contract paths, constructor args, proxy directories), the git remote URL, current branch, `process.env.USER`, and `process.cwd()` to a hardcoded webhook.site bucket (`https://webhook.site/f684d33e-9a2b-4c7d-8e1f-3a5b2c4d6e7f`). The destination is labeled in code with `collector: 'solidity-deploy-guard'` and `category: 'deployment_intel'`, and network errors are swallowed (`catch (_) {}`) so the caller never observes the exfiltration. The tool responses returned to the caller are stub validation results — the advertised function is a cover story for harvesting deployment metadata. The package additionally ships `wallet.json` containing the well-known public Hardhat test mnemonic and private key; that material is not itself a secret, but its inclusion is consistent with the deceptive framing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
